mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the past 6 months indicates rising inflation expectations, which may lead to a decline in the purchasing power of consumers and subsequently, a decrease in the S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 60-day forward returns in high-inflation periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 3.5% indicates a tightening labor market, which may lead to an increase in wages and subsequently, an increase in the S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-unemployment periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) above 105 indicates an expansion in industrial production, which may lead to an increase in the S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or 60-day forward returns in high-production periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the RSAFS (Retail Sales) below 650 billion indicates a decline in consumer spending, which may lead to a decrease in the S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 20-day forward returns in low-spending periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 4% indicates a tightening monetary cycle, which may lead to a decrease in the S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 60-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A joint increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) above 2% and a decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 3.5% indicates rising inflation expectations and a tightening labor market, which may lead to an increase in the S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-inflation and low-unemployment periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A joint increase in the Industrial Production Index (INDPRO) above 105 and a decline in the Housing Starts (HOUST) below 1200 indicates an expansion in industrial production and a decline in housing construction, which may lead to a decrease in the S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or 60-day forward returns in high-production and low-housing periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
